commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: feat: add WhatsApp read-only mode
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a true read-only mode for WhatsApp channels so OpenClaw can ingest and analyze inbound WhatsApp messages without ever sending outbound WhatsApp traffic.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#71166.

## Problem

WhatsApp could emit automatic outbound messages during inbound handling, including access-not-configured notices, pairing prompts, replies, read receipts, reactions, polls, typing indicators, and other transport-level sends.

That made it unsafe to attach WhatsApp in observation-only or production-adjacent setups, because there was no hard configuration-level guarantee that OpenClaw would stay silent.

## Fix

This PR adds `readOnly: true` support for WhatsApp at both channel and per-account config scopes, then enforces it inside the WhatsApp transport as a hard outbound gate.

When enabled, WhatsApp still receives and processes inbound messages, but outbound actions are dropped before they can leave the system.

Covered outbound paths include:

- text and media sends
- polls
- reactions
- typing/composing indicators
- inbound reply/send API closures
- pairing replies
- read receipts
- connect presence

## Testing

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions --pretty false`
- Targeted WhatsApp tests: 4 files, 48 tests

## Notes

This is intentionally enforced at the transport layer so accidental replies from agents, default system notices, or edge-case error handling cannot bypass read-only mode.
target.author: udaymanish6

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: pfrederiksen

target.title: fix(gateway): warn for insecure Control UI access
target.body:
## Summary
- Emit a gateway startup warning when Control UI is enabled on non-loopback HTTP with device auth still required
- Keep loopback, TLS, trusted-proxy auth mode, disabled Control UI, and `dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth` cases quiet
- Pass resolved Control UI/auth state into post-attach startup logging so the warning reflects the actual server mode after startup
- Add startup-log and post-attach regression tests for warning and non-warning cases
- Tighten loopback-origin handling and mobile pairing cleartext URL validation after rebasing onto current main

Fixes #71669

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Control UI startup logging either missed the insecure non-loopback HTTP/device-auth warning or warned in safe resolved modes such as trusted-proxy. The patch logs the warning only when the resolved startup state would actually reject direct non-localhost HTTP with `DEVICE_IDENTITY_REQUIRED`.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout for PR #73163 on Linux/Node 22 at head `fb7aa0d9b791937daa1bcfdd8d444f4ec4863b30`, using the real `logGatewayStartup` implementation from `src/gateway/server-startup-log.ts`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `node --import tsx tmp/pr73163-proof.mjs` from the patched checkout. The script exercised the production startup logger for remote plain HTTP, loopback HTTP, TLS, and trusted-proxy cases.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the after-fix command:

  ```text
  $ node --import tsx tmp/pr73163-proof.mjs
  remote plain-http warning emitted: true
  remote warning text: ⚠️  Control UI requires a secure browser context (HTTPS or localhost). Direct non-localhost HTTP connections will be rejected with DEVICE_IDENTITY_REQUIRED. Use HTTPS/Tailscale Serve, access via localhost or an SSH tunnel, configure an HTTPS reverse proxy, or set gateway.controlUi.dangerouslyDisableDeviceAuth=true as a break-glass option.
  loopback warning count: 0
  tls warning count: 0
  trusted-proxy warning count: 0
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** Remote non-loopback plain HTTP with token/device auth emits the secure-context warning, while loopback, TLS, and trusted-proxy modes stay quiet.
- **What was not tested:** A full live gateway restart was not performed for this proof; this proof directly exercises the production startup logger with resolved startup state, and the focused Vitest/format checks below remain supplemental.

## Additional current-head gateway/pairing proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Latest head also tightens Control UI browser-origin handling and mobile pairing cleartext URL validation. Forged non-local loopback origins should be rejected before pairing auth, local loopback should still work, tailnet `ws://` mobile pairing should be rejected with the Tailscale Serve guidance, and configured public `wss://` pairing should still work.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout for PR #73163 on Linux/Node 22 at head `fb7aa0d9b791937daa1bcfdd8d444f4ec4863b30`, using production `checkBrowserOrigin` and `resolvePairingSetupFromConfig`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `pnpm exec tsx --no-warnings -e '...'` proof script from the patched checkout that imported the production helpers and exercised forged loopback origin, local loopback origin, tailnet `ws://` pairing, and public `wss://` pairing.
- **Evidence after fix:**

  ```text
  forged loopback origin ok: false reason: origin not allowed
  local loopback origin ok: true matched: private-same-origin
  tailnet ws pairing ok: false error contains serve: true
  public wss pairing ok: true source: gateway.remote.url
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The forged remote loopback origin is rejected, legitimate local loopback still passes, cleartext tailnet mobile pairing returns the secure Tailscale Serve/Funnel guidance, and `wss://` public pairing remains accepted.
- **What was not tested:** A browser-driven Control UI login and live mobile app pairing flow were not run; this proof uses the production helper paths and the focused gateway/pairing tests below.

## Validation
- `CI=true pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/gateway/server-startup-log.ts src/gateway/server-startup-log.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server.impl.ts CHANGELOG.md`
- `CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-startup-log.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/gateway/origin-check.test.ts src/gateway/server.auth.browser-hardening.test.ts src/pairing/setup-code.test.ts` — 58 tests passed
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm check:changelog-attributions`
- `node --import tsx tmp/pr73163-proof.mjs`
- gateway/pairing proof script above
- `git diff --check`

Note: a local targeted `pnpm exec oxlint` run on these touched files hit pre-existing `no-underscore-dangle` findings for exported test helpers (`__testing`, `__resetModelCatalogCacheForTest`), so I did not count that as a passing validation gate.
target.author: pfrederiksen
